channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A$AP Rocky's friend testifies that the rapper fired a prop gun, not a real firearm, in 2021 shooting
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A$AP Rocky's longtime friend and collaborator testified at his Los Angeles trial Friday that the hip-hop star fired a starter pistol that he carried as a prop during a scuffle with a former friend on a Hollywood street in 2021.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California approves $50M to protect immigrants and defend state against Trump administration
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed laws Friday setting aside $50 million to help the state protect its policies from challenges by the Trump administration and defend immigrants amid the president's mass-deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard died, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board died, authorities said. Mike Salerno, a spokesperson for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 19 states sue to stop DOGE from accessing Americans' personal data
item.description: Nineteen Democratic attorneys general sued President Donald Trump on Friday to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Intoxicated Virginia man allegedly breaks into Bible study session thinking it was human trafficking operation
item.description: A Virginia man was arrested after breaking into a Bible study session he thought was a human trafficking operations, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal prisons being used to detain people arrested in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump’s administration is using federal prisons to detain some people arrested in its immigration crackdown, the federal Bureau of Prisons said Friday, returning to a strategy that drew allegations of mistreatment during his first term.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aircraft matching description of missing Alaska plane found, Coast Guard says
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard in Alaska said Friday afternoon that it had located an aircraft that matches the description of the Nome-bound plane that went missing after takeoff with 10 onboard on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USPS warns of romance scams ahead of Valentine’s Day
item.description: The United States Postal service is warning consumers about romance scams that target people seeking companionship ahead of Valentine's Day. USPS and the Postal Inspection Service says these schemes involve fraudsters targeting people in hopes of causing serious financial and emotional harm.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says US Steel will get investment from Nippon Steel, instead of being bought by it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday suggested that Nippon Steel would no longer buy U.S. Steel as planned, but the Japanese company would instead invest in the symbolically important American business. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on millions of low-value packages from China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has paused imposing tariffs on small-value packages arriving from China, apparently to give federal agencies time to sort out how to process millions of such shipments that have come through the U.S. border every day without paying taxes.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WGN 9

item.title: Woman is accused of stealing from a TV reporter found dead at his hotel before the Super Bowl
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police are investigating the death of a television reporter who was in New Orleans to cover the Super Bowl and on Friday announced the arrest of a woman they say used his credit card.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department lays out plans for $7 billion-plus arms sale to Israel as Netanyahu visits DC
item.description: The State Department has told Congress that it plans to sell more than $7 billion in weapons to Israel, including thousands of bombs and missiles, just two days after President Donald Trump met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Claims about USAID funding are spreading online. Many are not based on facts
item.description: As the Trump administration moves to dismantle the U.S. Agency for International Development, false and misleading information is being pushed on social media to support the change, much of it spread by the administration and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Priest attacked during service in Washington Catholic church, says Holy Spirit helped him respond
item.description: A priest is asking for prayers for a man who assaulted him earlier this week during a Washington church service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alaska Legislature asks Trump to retain Denali's name instead of change it to Mount McKinley
item.description: The Alaska Legislature has passed a resolution urging President Donald Trump to reverse course and retain the name of North America’s tallest peak as Denali rather than change it to Mount McKinley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks offering free coffee Monday after the Super Bowl: How to get yours
item.description: Starbucks is offering a free pick-me-up on Monday for football fans who might be staying up late celebrating (or fuming over) the results of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Naval Academy closing DEI offices to align with Trump executive orders: memo
item.description: The head of the U.S. Naval Academy says the school is closing DEI offices and ending DEI-related contracts to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Connecticut's Bradley International Airport experiencing air traffic controller shortage
item.description: Numbers obtained by NewsNation affiliate WTNH show that one senator is quite concerned about airline safety.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Derderian, NewsNation

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump executive order to deport Hamas sympathizers on campuses prompts student newspaper to delete names
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order to cancel Hamas sympathizers' student visas prompted Purdue University's student newspaper, the Exponent, to vow to delete names and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4
